channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: According to the latest update from Channels Television, Lebanon and Israel have agreed to extend their ceasefire for another 45 days even though there was small flare-up of violence. The US State Dep...

item.title: UK markets react sharply as Labour paves way for Andy Burnham to challenge Starmer
item.description: British financial markets experienced significant declines as Labour Party officials approved Andy Burnham’s bid to contest the party preselection for a forthcoming by-election. Burnham, the left-wing...

item.title: The Romanov Sisters Shaved Their Heads Due to Measles in 1917
item.description: In early 1917, during the final years of the Romanov dynasty, the four daughters of Tsar Nicholas II—Olga, Tatiana, Maria, and Anastasia—contracted measles, which caused significant hair loss. As a pr...

item.title: Lekgwathi praises Pirates’ scouting and goal-scoring as team nears league triumph
item.description: As Orlando Pirates edge closer to claiming the league championship, club legend Lucky Lekgwathi has highlighted the critical role of scouting and prolific goal-scoring in their success. Lekgwathi, who...

item.title: Zanzibar Women Empowered Through Clove Farming in PAMOJA Programme
item.description: Zanzibar is pushing for inclusive growth, gender equality, youth jobs and climate-smart agriculture through the World Bank-funded PAMOJA programme, valued at around $104 million (over Sh262 billion). ...

item.title: Kenyan Nurse in US Found Dead a Day Before Daughters' Graduation
item.description: Jessica Akersid Omoke, a 51-year-old Kenyan nurse working in Minnesota, was tragically found dead near her last known location in Maple Grove, Minnesota. She had been reported missing on Wednesday, Ma...

item.title: Man dies in shark attack near Rottnest Island, Western Australia
item.description: A 38-year-old man tragically lost his life after being attacked by a shark on Saturday morning near Horseshoe Reef, close to Rottnest Island, approximately 31 kilometres west of Perth, Western Austral...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch Receives Praise from US Rappers Nicki Minaj and Azealia Banks
item.description: Kemi Badenoch, leader of the UK Conservative Party, has received unexpected commendation from American rappers Nicki Minaj and Azealia Banks. Minaj praised Badenoch on social media, comparing her to M...

item.title: Canada's Complex and Slow Deportation Process for Senior Iranian Officials
item.description: Canada has struggled to deport senior Iranian officials despite announcing a ban following Iran’s crackdown on protesters in 2022. The case of Abbas Omidi, a geologist who worked in Iran’s Ministry of...

item.title: Supreme Court of Canada Recognizes Civil Claims for Intimate Partner Violence
item.description: In a historic decision, the Supreme Court of Canada has formally recognised a new civil tort for intimate partner violence, allowing victims to seek financial compensation for abuse suffered in intima...

item.title: Captain Zanele Madiba becomes first woman to command SAS Amatola in South African Navy
item.description: Captain Zanele Madiba has made history as the first woman to command a Valour Class Frigate in the South African Navy, taking charge of the SAS Amatola during the Change of Command Parade at Naval Bas...

item.title: Cybercriminal Twins Plead Guilty After Their Microsoft Teams Recording Reveals Attacks on Government Databases
item.description: This week in cybersecurity news, two brothers, Muneeb and Sohaib Akhter, pleaded guilty for destroying 96 government databases after being fired from their jobs at federal contractor Opexus. Their pla...

item.title: Tanzania Sees Strong Rise in Lorry Demand Reaching 15,000 Units Annually Due to Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania economy is growing fast and this is causing big increase in need for lorries. Now the country requires about 15,000 lorries each year to support trade, construction, mining and infrastructure...

item.title: Early 20th Century Proposal to Deploy Canadian Troops to India Under British Rule
item.description: At the turn of the 20th century, a proposal emerged within the British Empire to deploy Canadian troops to India for training and frontier defence. The scheme, known as Sir Frederick Borden’s Scheme, ...

item.title: Mike Pezzullo advocates for increased defence readiness despite past controversies
item.description: Michael Pezzullo, former head of the Department of Home Affairs, has publicly expressed a strong desire for military engagement and increased defence preparedness. The article portrays Pezzullo as a c...

item.title: Restoring boreal forests in northern Europe to support reindeer herding and Sámi livelihoods
item.description: Political debates in Sweden and the European Union regarding forest management have reached a deadlock between increasing wood production and prioritising nature conservation and carbon storage. Howev...

item.title: Athena Retains Ring of Honor Women’s Championship, Aims for Historic Reign
item.description: Athena continued her dominant run in professional wrestling by retaining the Ring of Honor Women’s World Championship at the Supercard of Honor event. The championship was contested in a challenging S...

item.title: Martha Karua Criticises Rising Fuel Prices Under Ruto, Draws Parallels to Moi Era
item.description: Martha Karua, the leader of the People’s Liberation Party (PLP), has drawn sharp comparisons between President William Ruto’s administration and the government of former President Daniel Arap Moi, fol...

item.title: Cayetano’s Use of Faith in Politics Raises Questions on Accountability
item.description: The article critically examines the public persona and political career of Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano, highlighting the apparent contradictions between his professed Christian faith and his ...

item.title: PCOS Now Called PMOS to Reflect Hormonal and Metabolic Health in Women
item.description: For long, many women wey dey suffer from PCOS dey confuse because di name no really represent wetin dey happen for dia body. As of May 2026, di medical world don officially rename di condition to PMOS...
